steps.title: Identify what makes hDNA distinctive
steps.content: The decisive signal is the phrase “offers unique benefits, such as the ability to ascertain the biogeographical origins of organisms.” The word “unique” directly indicates hDNA provides capabilities that other methods do not.

steps.title: Match the unique benefits to the choices
steps.content:
The matching choice states hDNA “may yield insights that other types of genomic data cannot.” This directly captures the passage's key claim that hDNA “offers unique benefits.” The other three choices either introduce unsupported comparisons (A), contradict the passage's explanation for underuse (B), or make unsupported generalizations (C).

D is correct.
